fastqs.desc: A FOFN with sample names and paths to FASTQ/FASTAs to process

r1.desc: First set of compressed (gzip) paired-end FASTQ reads (requires --r2 a

r2.desc: Second set of compressed (gzip) paired-end FASTQ reads (requires --r1

ont.desc: Treat `--se` as long reads for analysis. (requires --sample)

hybrid.desc: Treat `--se` as long reads for hybrid assembly.  (requires --r1, --r2,

sample.desc: Sample name to use for the input sequences

accessions.desc: A file containing ENA/SRA Experiment accessions or NCBI Assembly acces

accession.desc: Sample name to use for the input sequences

assembly.desc: A assembled genome in compressed FASTA format. (requires --sample)

datasets.desc: The path to datasets that have already been set up

species.desc: Name of species for species-specific dataset to use

coverage.desc: Reduce samples to a given coverage

genome_size.desc: Expected genome size (bp) for all samples, a value of '0' will disable

available_datasets.desc: Print a list of available datasets found based on location given by `-

skip_fastq_check.desc: Skip minimum requirement checks for input FASTQs

min_basepairs.desc: The minimum amount of basepairs required to continue downstream analys

min_reads.desc: The minimum amount of reads required to continue downstream analyses.

min_coverage.desc: The minimum amount of coverage required to continue downstream analyse

min_proportion.desc: The minimum proportion of basepairs for paired-end reads to continue d

min_genome_size.desc: The minimum estimated genome size allowed for the input sequence to co

max_genome_size.desc: The maximum estimated genome size allowed for the input sequence to co

no_cache.desc: Skip caching the assembly summary file from ncbi-genome-download

skip_qc.desc: The QC step will be skipped and it will be assumed the inputs sequence

skip_qc_plots.desc: QC Plot creation by FastQC or Nanoplot will be skipped

skip_error_correction.desc: FLASH error correction of reads will be skipped.

adapters.desc: A FASTA file containing adapters to remove

adapter_k.desc: Kmer length used for finding adapters.

phix_k.desc: Kmer length used for finding phiX174.

ktrim.desc: Trim reads to remove bases matching reference kmers

mink.desc: Look for shorter kmers at read tips down to this length, when k-trimmi

hdist.desc: Maximum Hamming distance for ref kmers (subs only)

tpe.desc: When kmer right-trimming, trim both reads to the minimum length of eit

tbo.desc: Trim adapters based on where paired reads overlap

qtrim.desc: Trim read ends to remove bases with quality below trimq.

trimq.desc: Regions with average quality BELOW this will be trimmed if qtrim is se

maq.desc: Reads with average quality (after trimming) below this will be discard

minlength.desc: Reads shorter than this after trimming will be discarded

ftm.desc: If positive, right-trim length to be equal to zero, modulo this number

tossjunk.desc: Discard reads with invalid characters as bases

qout.desc: PHRED offset to use for output FASTQs

maxcor.desc: Max number of corrections within a 20bp window

sampleseed.desc: Set to a positive number to use as the random number generator seed fo

ont_minlength.desc: ONT Reads shorter than this will be discarded

ont_minqual.desc: Minimum average read quality filter of ONT reads

shovill_assembler.desc: Assembler to be used by Shovill

dragonflye_assembler.desc: Assembler to be used by Dragonflye

use_unicycler.desc: Use unicycler for paired end assembly

contig_namefmt.desc: Format of contig FASTA IDs in 'printf' style

no_stitch.desc: Disable read stitching for paired-end reads

min_polish_size.desc: Contigs shorter than this value (bp) will not be polished using Pilon

min_component_size.desc: Graph dead ends smaller than this size (bp) will be removed from the f

min_dead_end_size.desc: Graph dead ends smaller than this size (bp) will be removed from the f

medaka_model.desc: The model to use for Medaka polishing

medaka_steps.desc: The number of Medaka polishing steps to conduct

racon_steps.desc: The number of Racon polishing steps to conduct

no_rotate.desc: Do not rotate completed replicons to start at a standard gene

reassemble.desc: If reads were simulated, they will be used to create a new assembly.

run_checkm.desc: Run CheckM in the assembly QC step

checkm_unique.desc: Minimum number of unique phylogenetic markers required to use lineage-

checkm_multi.desc: Maximum number of multi-copy phylogenetic markers before defaulting to

aai_strain.desc: AAI threshold used to identify strain heterogeneity

checkm_length.desc: Percent overlap between target and query

full_tree.desc: Use the full tree (requires ~40GB of memory) for determining lineage o

skip_pseudogene_correction.desc: Skip identification and filtering of pseudogene

checkm_ali.desc: Generate HMMER alignment file for each bin

checkm_nt.desc: Generate nucleotide gene sequences for each bin

force_domain.desc: Use domain-level sets for all bins

no_refinement.desc: Do not perform lineage-specific marker set refinement

skip_adj_correction.desc: Do not exclude adjacent marker genes when estimating contamination

contig_thresholds.desc: Comma-separated list of contig length thresholds

nogenes.desc: Do not add 'gene' features for each 'CDS' feature

rnammer.desc: Prefer RNAmmer over Barrnap for rRNA prediction

rfam.desc: Enable searching for ncRNAs with Infernal+Rfam

skip_prodigal_tf.desc: If a Prodigal training file was found, it will not be used

mapqual.desc: Minimum read mapping quality to consider

mincov.desc: Minimum site depth to for calling alleles

bwaopt.desc: Extra BWA MEM options, eg. -x pacbio

random_tie_break.desc: On references with matching distances, randomly select one.

disable_auto_variants.desc: Disable automatic selection of reference genome based on Mash distance

mash_sketch.desc: Sketch size. Each sketch will have at most this many non-redundant min

sourmash_scale.desc: Choose number of hashes as 1 in FRACTION of input k-mers

no_winner_take_all.desc: Disable winner-takes-all strategy for identity estimates

keep_unmapped_reads.desc: Keep unmapped reads, this does not affect variant calling.

bwa_n.desc: Maximum number of alignments to output in the XA tag for reads paired

amr_plus.desc: Add the plus genes to the report

amr_report_common.desc: Suppress proteins common to a taxonomy group

amr_organism.desc: Taxonomy group: Campylobacter, Escherichia, Klebsiella Salmonella, Sta

amr_ident_min.desc: Minimum identity for nucleotide hit (0..1). -1 means use a curated thr

amr_coverage_min.desc: Minimum coverage of the reference protein

amr_translation_table.desc: NCBI genetic code for translated BLAST

mlst_nucmer_breaklen.desc: Value to use for -breaklen when running nucmer

mlst_assembly_cov.desc: Target read coverage when sampling reads for assembly

mlst_min_scaff_depth.desc: Minimum number of read pairs needed as evidence for scaffold link betw

mlst_spades_options.desc: Extra options to pass to Spades assembler

mlst_assembled_threshold.desc: If proportion of gene assembled (regardless of into how many contigs)

mlst_gene_nt_extend.desc: Max number of nucleotides to extend ends of gene matches to look for s

mlst_unique_threshold.desc: If proportion of bases in gene assembled more than once is <= this val

mlst_ariba_no_clean.desc: Do not clean up intermediate files created by Ariba.

max_target_seqs.desc: Maximum number of aligned sequences to keep

nucmer_breaklen.desc: Value to use for -breaklen when running nucmer

assembly_cov.desc: Target read coverage when sampling reads for assembly

min_scaff_depth.desc: Minimum number of read pairs needed as evidence for scaffold link betw

spades_options.desc: Extra options to pass to Spades assembler

assembled_threshold.desc: If proportion of gene assembled (regardless of into how many contigs)

gene_nt_extend.desc: Max number of nucleotides to extend ends of gene matches to look for s

unique_threshold.desc: If proportion of bases in gene assembled more than once is <= this val

ariba_no_clean.desc: Do not clean up intermediate files created by Ariba.

outdir.desc: Base directory to write results to

run_name.desc: Name of the directory to hold results

skip_compression.desc: Ouput files will not be compressed

max_retry.desc: Maximum times to retry a process before allowing it to fail.

max_cpus.desc: Maximum number of CPUs that can be requested for any single job.

max_memory.desc: Maximum amount of memory (in GB) that can be requested for any single

max_time.desc: Maximum amount of time (in minutes) that can be requested for any sing

max_downloads.desc: Maximum number of samples to download at a time

nfconfig.desc: A Nextflow compatible config file for custom profiles, loaded last and

publish_dir_mode.desc: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.

infodir.desc: Directory to keep pipeline Nextflow logs and reports.

force.desc: Nextflow will overwrite existing output files.

cleanup_workdir.desc: After Bactopia is successfully executed, the `work` directory will be

condadir.desc: Directory to Nextflow should use for Conda environments

registry.desc: Docker registry to pull containers from.

singularity_cache.desc: Directory where remote Singularity images are stored.

singularity_pull_docker_container.desc: Instead of directly downloading Singularity images for use with Singul

queue.desc: Comma-separated name of the queue(s) to be used by a job scheduler (e.

disable_scratch.desc: All intermediate files created on worker nodes of will be transferred

aws_region.desc: AWS Region to be used by Nextflow

aws_volumes.desc: Volumes to be mounted from the EC2 instance to the Docker container

aws_cli_path.desc: Path to the AWS CLI for Nextflow to use.

aws_upload_storage_class.desc: The S3 storage slass to use for storing files on S3

aws_max_parallel_transfers.desc: The number of parallele transfers between EC2 and S3

aws_delay_between_attempts.desc: The duration of sleep (in seconds) between each transfer between EC2 a

aws_max_transfer_attempts.desc: The maximum number of times to retry transferring a file between EC2 a

aws_max_retry.desc: The maximum number of times to retry a process on AWS Batch

aws_ecr_registry.desc: The ECR registry containing Bactopia related containers.

monochrome_logs.desc: Do not use coloured log outputs.

nfdir.desc: Print directory Nextflow has pulled Bactopia to

sleep_time.desc: The amount of time (seconds) Nextflow will wait after setting up datas

validate_params.desc: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

wf.desc: Specify which workflow or Bactopia Tool to execute

list_wfs.desc: List the available workflows and Bactopia Tools to use with '--wf'

use_prank.default: false // Use PRANK instead of MAFFT fr core-genome alignment

cd.default: 99 // percentage of isolates a gene must be in to be core [99]

ap.default: false // allow paralogs in core alignment

blastp_outfmt.default: "sseqid qseqid pident qlen slen length nident positive mismatch gapopen gaps qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"

tblastx_outfmt.default: "sseqid qseqid pident qlen slen length nident positive mismatch gapopen gaps qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"

blastx_outfmt.default: "sseqid qseqid pident qlen slen length nident positive mismatch gapopen gaps qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"

blastn_outfmt.default: "sseqid qseqid pident qlen slen length nident positive mismatch gapopen gaps qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"

tblastn_outfmt.default: "sseqid qseqid pident qlen slen length nident positive mismatch gapopen gaps qstart qend sstart send evalue bitscore"

a.default: false // Count all differences not just [AGTC]

b.default: false // Keep top left corner cell

csv.default: false // Output CSV instead of TSV

k.default: false // Keep case, don't uppercase all letters

alrt.default: 1000 // SH-like approximate likelihood ratio test (SH-aLRT) replicates

asr.default: false // Ancestral state reconstruction by empirical Bayes

steps.default: '50,60,70,80,90,95,98'	// % identity thresholds to use for pangenome construction

features.default: 'CDS' //	choose features to use for pangenome construction. Multiple may be entered, separated by a comma

para_off.default: false // switch off paralog identification [default: off]

pan_opt.default: '' // Allows the params below to be given as a string
